# Using Theme Studio CSS Export in Next.js + Tailwind

## What the export looks like

The exported file produces bare HSL channel values (no `hsl()` wrapper) inside `@layer base`:

```css
/* Generated by Theme Studio */

@layer base {
  :root {
    --background: 0 0% 100%;
    --foreground: 222 47% 11%;
    --primary: 221 83% 53%;
    --radius: 0.5rem;
    /* ... */
  }

  .dark {
    --background: 222 47% 11%;
    --foreground: 0 0% 100%;
    /* ... */
  }
}
```

This is the **official shadcn/ui theming pattern** — not a workaround. shadcn components are built expecting exactly these variables, so the export is a direct drop-in.

---

## Step 1 — Add the file to your project

Place the exported file somewhere like `src/styles/theme.css`.

---

## Step 2 — Import it in your global CSS

### Tailwind v3

```css
@import "./theme.css";

@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;
```

### Tailwind v4

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./theme.css";
```

---

## Step 3 — Wire up Tailwind to use the variables

### Tailwind v3 — `tailwind.config.ts`

Map the CSS variables to Tailwind color names using the `hsl()` wrapper:

```ts
import type { Config } from "tailwindcss";

const config: Config = {
  darkMode: "class", // matches the .dark selector in the export
  theme: {
    extend: {
      colors: {
        background: "hsl(var(--background))",
        foreground: "hsl(var(--foreground))",
        primary: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--primary))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--primary-foreground))",
        },
        secondary: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--secondary))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--secondary-foreground))",
        },
        muted: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--muted))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--muted-foreground))",
        },
        accent: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--accent))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--accent-foreground))",
        },
        destructive: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--destructive))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--destructive-foreground))",
        },
        border: "hsl(var(--border))",
        input: "hsl(var(--input))",
        ring: "hsl(var(--ring))",
        card: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--card))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--card-foreground))",
        },
        popover: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--popover))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--popover-foreground))",
        },
        // Extended tokens
        brand: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--brand))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--brand-foreground))",
        },
        success: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--success))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--success-foreground))",
        },
        warning: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--warning))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--warning-foreground))",
        },
        info: {
          DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--info))",
          foreground: "hsl(var(--info-foreground))",
        },
      },
      borderRadius: {
        sm: "var(--radius-sm)",
        DEFAULT: "var(--radius)",
        md: "var(--radius-md)",
        lg: "var(--radius-lg)",
        xl: "var(--radius-xl)",
      },
    },
  },
};

export default config;
```

> Radius variables are used **without** `hsl()` since they are length values (`rem`), not colors.

### Tailwind v4 — `globals.css`

With Tailwind v4, use `@theme inline` instead of `tailwind.config.ts`:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "./theme.css";

@theme inline {
  --color-background: hsl(var(--background));
  --color-foreground: hsl(var(--foreground));
  --color-primary: hsl(var(--primary));
  --color-primary-foreground: hsl(var(--primary-foreground));
  --color-secondary: hsl(var(--secondary));
  --color-secondary-foreground: hsl(var(--secondary-foreground));
  --color-muted: hsl(var(--muted));
  --color-muted-foreground: hsl(var(--muted-foreground));
  --color-accent: hsl(var(--accent));
  --color-accent-foreground: hsl(var(--accent-foreground));
  --color-destructive: hsl(var(--destructive));
  --color-destructive-foreground: hsl(var(--destructive-foreground));
  --color-border: hsl(var(--border));
  --color-input: hsl(var(--input));
  --color-ring: hsl(var(--ring));
  --color-card: hsl(var(--card));
  --color-card-foreground: hsl(var(--card-foreground));
  --color-brand: hsl(var(--brand));
  --color-brand-foreground: hsl(var(--brand-foreground));
  --radius-md: var(--radius);
}
```

> The CSS variable file itself stays identical between v3 and v4 — only how Tailwind consumes it changes.

---

## Step 4 — Use them in components

```tsx
// Tailwind utility classes
<div className="bg-background text-foreground">
  <button className="bg-primary text-primary-foreground rounded-md">
    Click me
  </button>
</div>

// Opacity modifiers work out of the box with the hsl(var()) pattern
<div className="bg-primary/50" />

// Raw CSS variables also work directly
<div style={{ color: "hsl(var(--brand))" }} />
```

---

## Step 5 — Dark mode

Since the export uses the `.dark` class selector, toggle it on `<html>`:

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
<html className={isDark ? "dark" : ""}>
```

shadcn components pick this up automatically — no additional configuration needed.

---

## Why this is best practice

| Benefit | Reason |
|---|---|
| **shadcn compatible** | Components expect exactly these variable names — it's a direct drop-in |
| **Single source of truth** | Change a token once, every component updates |
| **Zero-cost dark mode** | Variable swap via CSS class, no JS computation |
| **Opacity modifiers** | `bg-primary/50` works because of the `hsl(var())` pattern |
| **Framework agnostic** | The CSS file works in any project, not just Next.js |
